concerns: Risk note is accurate: the US-004 test at tests/us-004/responsive-shell.test.ts does NOT assert specific footer text content — it only checks the footer element exists, DOM order, and semantic landmarks. So the identified risk of breaking US-004 tests by changing footer text is actually lower than stated. Update the risk assessment to reflect this.

Plan step 2 mentions using --font-size-xs but the current footer already uses --font-size-sm. Verify the intent — switching to xs may reduce readability on mobile. Consider keeping --font-size-sm or at minimum documenting the rationale for the size change.

The plan should explicitly mention that the existing footer text ('All analysis runs locally. No data leaves your browser.') will be refactored into the four discrete statements. The current single string partially overlaps with AC item 5 ('States all analysis runs locally') — make clear this is a restructure, not a net-new addition, to avoid duplicating the message.
